claims.evidence_text: Site mean HI ranged from 0.29 at Temora 2014 and Yarrawonga 2018 to 0.47 at Loxton in 2018.
claims.claim_summary: Site-mean harvest index of early-sown winter wheat ranged from 0.29 to 0.47 across environments.

claims.evidence_text: the maximum of 0.56 reported by Unkovich et al. (2010)
claims.claim_summary: Maximum wheat harvest index reported is about 0.56; well-managed spring cultivars commonly reach ~0.45.

claims.evidence_text: KW(mg) ... DS Bennett 27.3 c ... Kittyhawk 39.8 a ... (individual grain weight measured by weighing 200 grains)
claims.claim_summary: Individual (thousand) kernel weight of winter wheat cultivars ranged from ~27.3 to ~39.8 mg per grain across genotypes and environments.

claims.evidence_text: Hght(cm) ... DS Bennett ... 58 b ... Yarrawonga 2017 DS Bennett ... 98 a
claims.claim_summary: Winter wheat plant height ranged from about 58 cm to 98 cm across cultivars and environments.

claims.evidence_text: Temperatures below −4°C are likely to cause stem frost damage and occurred at these sites during stem elongation.
claims.claim_summary: Wheat stem frost damage during stem elongation is likely when temperatures fall below -4 C; pre-heading stem damage can occur approaching -6 C.

claims.evidence_text: Heat stress events no. of days temperature > 32°C (Max Temperature) ... temperatures above 32°C were common at Loxton
claims.claim_summary: Days above 32 C were used to define heat-stress events for wheat around heading/flowering.

claims.evidence_text: GYs were measured by machine harvest of the inside four rows of six row plots and are reported at 12.5% moisture content.
claims.claim_summary: Wheat grain yield reported at 12.5% grain moisture content.

claims.evidence_text: two plant density treatments (low and high) targeting 50 and 150 plants/m 2 , respectively.
claims.claim_summary: Winter wheat plant density treatments targeted 50 (low) and 150 (high) plants/m2.

claims.evidence_text: two nitrogen timings (seedbed N and deferred N) ensuring either adequate N supply at sowing or deferred until early stem elongation (development stage 30–31)
claims.claim_summary: Nitrogen applied either at seedbed (sowing) or deferred to early stem elongation (DC30-31); deferred N reduced stem frost damage by ~7%.

claims.evidence_text: All cultivars have an obligate requirement for vernalization (winter wheat) and weak photoperiod sensitivity.
claims.claim_summary: The winter wheat cultivars studied have an obligate vernalization requirement and weak photoperiod sensitivity.

claims.evidence_text: All cultivars have an obligate requirement for vernalization (winter wheat) and weak photoperiod sensitivity.
claims.claim_summary: The winter wheat cultivars studied showed weak photoperiod sensitivity.

claims.evidence_text: DM ranged from 2.7t/ha at Yarrawonga 2018 to 12.3t/ha at Temora in 2015.
claims.claim_summary: Above-ground dry matter (biomass) at maturity ranged from 2.7 to 12.3 t/ha across environments.
